findings.description: _is_expired inverts the comparison (age < ttl), so fresh entries are reported expired and stale entries are served indefinitely.
pickle.loads on bytes fetched from Redis enables arbitrary code execution if the shared L2 store is poisoned or tampered with.
Using None as the miss sentinel means a legitimately cached None is treated as a miss, causing get_or_compute to recompute every call.
Mutations of _local and stats counters are unsynchronized, so concurrent access from multiple threads can race and corrupt counts/state.
stats.local_entries is updated only in set(), so it drifts after get()/invalidate() and is inconsistent with the len(_local) used in export_stats.
RedisClient.setex ignores ttl_seconds, so L2 entries never expire and the TTL contract is honored only at L1.
pickle.loads has no error handling; corrupt or non-picklable L2 payloads raise and propagate to the caller instead of degrading to a miss.
An L2 hit is counted only as stats.hits even though the L1 lookup just missed, so hit/miss ratios understate L1 misses.
Module-level logger is created but never used anywhere in the file.
